name: ws13: clawft-app — decide UnknownMode validation variant fate
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 1; §clawft-app deferred items).

## Problem / gap

`ValidationError::UnknownMode` is dead code — serde rejects out-of-set `supported_modes` values at parse time. Decision is open: wire a Rust-constructed-manifest check, or delete the variant.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Decide whether the variant covers Rust-constructed manifests or should be deleted.
- [ ] If kept: add a Rust-side validation path + unit test.
- [ ] If deleted: remove the variant and update tests/docs.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-app/src/validation.rs`.
- ROADMAP §"Review-deferred follow-ups" beads (<30 LoC).

name: ws13: clawft-app — add registry corruption quarantine path
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 2; §clawft-app deferred items).

## Problem / gap

Registry corruption recovery currently returns a JSON error to the caller. A quarantine path (rename to `apps.json.corrupt-<ts>`) plus backup/repair would let the daemon self-heal a damaged registry instead of erroring out.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] On JSON parse failure, rename `apps.json` to `apps.json.corrupt-<ts>` and start fresh.
- [ ] Unit test covers the rename + recovery path.
- [ ] Logs/telemetry surface the corruption event.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-app/src/registry.rs`.

name: ws13: clawft-app — emit lifecycle teardown tombstone on uninstall-while-enabled
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 3; §clawft-app deferred items).

## Problem / gap

`uninstall` while enabled does NOT yet run the ADR-015 §Lifecycle teardown (surfaces / subscriptions / affordances) because compositor-level hooks don't exist. Need to emit a tombstone the compositor can catch when the M1.6+ hooks land.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Uninstall on an enabled app records a tombstone event/result.
- [ ] Compositor consumers can subscribe to the tombstone.
- [ ] Test exercises uninstall-while-enabled and confirms tombstone emission.

## Dependencies

- ADR-015 lifecycle hook implementation (M1.6+).

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-app/src/lifecycle.rs`.

name: ws13: clawft-app — wire ADR-015 rule 6 once clawft-adapter exists
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 4; TODOs `clawft-app/src/lib.rs:13`, `validation.rs:172–178`).

## Problem / gap

ADR-015 rule 6 (permission ↔ adapter consistency check) is TODO'd until ADR-017/`clawft-adapter` lands. The `let _ = Permission::Camera;` placeholder is kept in scope. Governance at install time is the backstop today.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Implement rule 6 once `clawft-adapter` (or equivalent registry) exists.
- [ ] Add validation tests for permission/adapter mismatches.
- [ ] Remove the `let _ = Permission::Camera;` placeholder.

## Dependencies

- `clawft-adapter` crate (does not exist yet).
- ADR-017 finalisation.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-app/src/{lib.rs:13,validation.rs:172–178}`.

name: ws13: clawft-app — cover wasm to_toml_string failure path with negative test
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 5; orphaned/ambiguous: `ManifestParseError::Serialize` wasm32 path unverified).

## Problem / gap

The `web-time` switch handled `SystemTime` but not `toml::ser::Error`. The wasm32 serialize-failure path is unverified.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Add a negative test that triggers `ManifestParseError::Serialize` under wasm32.
- [ ] Confirm the failure surfaces cleanly without panic.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-app/src/manifest.rs`.

name: ws13: clawft-substrate — emit substrate/meta/adapter/<id>/health from each adapter
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 6; ADR-017 §7; `clawft-substrate/src/lib.rs:23`).

## Problem / gap

The `substrate/meta/adapter/<id>/health` topic is stubbed in the kernel adapter and not emitted anywhere else. Adapter health is invisible to surfaces today.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Each in-tree adapter (kernel/network/bluetooth/mesh/chain/mic) emits a health topic.
- [ ] Topic shape documented in ADR-017 alignment.
- [ ] Tests cover at least one adapter's health emissions.

## Dependencies

- Coordinates with task 23 (per-sensor healthcheck contract emitter).

## Notes

- ROADMAP §"Review-deferred follow-ups".

name: ws13: clawft-substrate — per-id Replace/Remove deltas on processes/services topics
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 7; `kernel.rs:13`).

## Problem / gap

Processes/services topics emit a whole-list `Replace` per tick. Per-pid / per-name `Replace` + `Remove` deltas are deferred to M1.6+ pending a daemon-side row-id contract.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Daemon RPC grows a row-id contract for processes/services.
- [ ] Adapter emits per-row Replace/Remove deltas.
- [ ] Tests confirm reduced delta size on steady-state ticks.

## Dependencies

- Daemon-side row-id contract change.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-substrate/src/kernel.rs`.

name: ws13: clawft-substrate — surface Subscription closed via adapter-health topic on teardown
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 8; deferred §clawft-substrate adapter-health).

## Problem / gap

No liveness ping or auto-reconnect signal — the drain task silently exits when the sender closes. Subscribers cannot tell the difference between idle and dead.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Drain-task exit emits a `Subscription closed` event on the adapter-health topic.
- [ ] Test exercises adapter teardown and asserts the emission.

## Dependencies

- Task 6 (adapter-health topic shape).

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-substrate/src/adapter.rs`, `snapshot.rs`.

name: ws13: clawft-substrate — migrate mic adapter to substrate/<node-id>/sensor/mic/{summary,pcm}
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 9; `JOURNALED-SENSOR-MIC.md`; `mic.rs:33,84,192–202`).

## Problem / gap

The in-tree `MicrophoneAdapter` still emits the legacy flat `substrate/sensor/mic` summary only — no `pcm` topic, no node-scoped path. ESP32-signed publish path is only declared in planning.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Mic adapter publishes under `substrate/<node-id>/sensor/mic/{summary,pcm}`.
- [ ] `pcm` ships as a windowed-`Append` topic.
- [ ] Cutover documented in `ADOPTION.md` (see task 29).

## Dependencies

- Resolution of legacy-flat vs node-scoped naming (task 29).
- ESP32 node signing prefix gate (task 24).

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-substrate/src/mic.rs`.

name: ws13: clawft-substrate — ship a second Characterization exemplar (Enumerated or Spectral)
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 10; sensor-framework deferred items).

## Problem / gap

Only `Characterization::Rate` is exercised (mic). No `Presence` / `Enumerated` / `Spectral` / `Identifying` adapter ships. Need at least one more exemplar to validate the framework.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Implement an `Enumerated` rfkill adapter or a `Spectral` FFT-mic.
- [ ] Tests cover the new characterization path.
- [ ] Adapter registered in the substrate lineup.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-substrate/src/physical.rs`.

name: ws13: clawft-substrate — implement cross-platform network/bluetooth or document Linux-only
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 11; cross-platform stubs).

## Problem / gap

`network` / `bluetooth` adapters return `absent` on non-Linux; macOS / Windows variants are unscheduled. Either ship them or document the Linux-only constraint.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Either implement macOS / Windows adapter variants, OR
- [ ] Update docs to clearly state Linux-only support and behaviour on other OSes.
- [ ] Surface non-Linux fallback in adapter-health (sysfs error cause).

## Dependencies

- Task 6 (adapter-health surfacing).

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-substrate/src/{network,bluetooth}.rs`.

name: ws13: clawft-surface — wire 13 stub-leaf canon primitives in the composer
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 12; `tree.rs:119,276`; `compose.rs:11,681,686`).

## Problem / gap

13 of the 21 canon leaves ship as M1.5 stub `TODO:` labels: Field, Toggle, Select, Slider, Plot, Media, Canvas, Foreign, Modal, Tabs, Sheet, Dock, Tree. Any non-switched IR node renders "TODO: <iri> not wired in M1.5". This is a substantial surface gap visible to any third-party app.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 13 stub-leaf primitives have working composer renderers (~80 LoC each).
- [ ] Each primitive has fixture coverage in admin or demo surfaces.
- [ ] Composer no longer emits TODO labels for canon IRIs.

## Dependencies

- May coordinate with task 18 (canon-types extraction) for cleaner architecture.

## Notes

- Significant effort (13 × ~80 LoC). Largest single bead in this WS.

name: ws13: clawft-surface — add .first/.last field-access shorthand support
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 13; deferred §clawft-surface; `lib.rs` header).

## Problem / gap

`.first` / `.last` as field-access shorthand is not supported (only function-call form). Documented in `lib.rs` header.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Parser accepts `$path.first` and `$path.last` as field-access shorthand.
- [ ] Evaluator returns the same result as the function-call form.
- [ ] Round-trip and eval tests cover both forms.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-surface/src/parse/expr.rs`, `eval.rs`.

name: ws13: clawft-surface — implement sort(list, key) ordering combinator
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 14; ADR-016 §5; deferred §clawft-surface).

## Problem / gap

`sort(list, key)` ordering combinator from ADR-016 §5 is not implemented in the evaluator or arity table.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Add `sort` to the static arity table.
- [ ] Implement evaluator branch with key-lambda binding.
- [ ] Tests cover sort-by-field and sort-by-derived-value.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-surface/src/{eval.rs,parse/expr.rs}`.

name: ws13: clawft-surface — accept scientific (1e5) and hex (0xff) number literals
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 15; deferred §clawft-surface).

## Problem / gap

Scientific (`1e5`) / hex (`0xff`) number literals are not accepted by the expression parser.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Parser accepts both literal forms.
- [ ] Tests confirm correct numeric value lowering.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-surface/src/parse/expr.rs`.

name: ws13: clawft-surface — parse [compositions.*] and expand in composer
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 16; deferred §clawft-surface).

## Problem / gap

User-defined compositions (`[compositions.*]`) are not parsed, so reusable surface fragments cannot be authored in TOML.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] TOML parser accepts `[compositions.*]` blocks.
- [ ] Composer expands referenced compositions during render.
- [ ] Round-trip test covers a multi-composition surface.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-surface/src/parse/toml.rs`.

name: ws13: clawft-surface — drop unused egui dep from Cargo.toml
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 17; orphaned/ambiguous list).

## Problem / gap

`clawft-surface/Cargo.toml` keeps `egui = "0.34"` with the comment "kept for now; can drop once eval.rs is fully UI-free." `eval.rs` no longer references egui — the dep can be dropped, unblocking the surface crate for non-egui consumers (browser canvas, terminal).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Remove the egui dependency from `clawft-surface/Cargo.toml`.
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh check` passes on native and wasi/browser targets.

## Dependencies

- Task 18 (canon-types extraction) may simplify this further but is not required.

## Notes

- Trivial (<5 LoC).

name: ws13: clawft-surface — extract canon types and move composer back to clawft-surface
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 18; deferred §clawft-surface; cross-cutting §3).

## Problem / gap

The composer runtime lives in `clawft-gui-egui::surface_host` rather than in `clawft-surface`. The previously-cyclic dep is broken (`f5e40c3`) by composer relocation — but the proper fix (extract canon types to a shared crate, move composer back) is unscheduled.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] New shared canon-types crate created and consumed by both `clawft-surface` and `clawft-gui-egui`.
- [ ] Composer runtime relocated into `clawft-surface`.
- [ ] No cyclic deps; gate passes.

## Dependencies

- Task 17 (drop egui dep) becomes natural after this lands.

## Notes

- Cross-cutting architecture work.

name: ws13: clawft-surface — replace 14-line src/substrate.rs shim with direct re-export
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 19; orphaned/ambiguous).

## Problem / gap

`clawft-surface/src/substrate.rs` is a 14-line shim re-exporting `OntologySnapshot` from `clawft_substrate::snapshot`. Decide: keep the indirection or fold into `lib.rs`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Either fold the shim into `lib.rs` or document why the indirection exists.
- [ ] Downstream consumers continue to compile.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-surface/src/substrate.rs`.

name: ws13: integration — wire real ADR-012 governance::Gate through Substrate::subscribe_adapter
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 20; `lifecycle.rs:11,72`; `clawft-substrate/src/lib.rs:19`; `adapter.rs:133`).

## Problem / gap

The real ADR-012 governance gate is M1.6+. `NoopGate` / `StrictGate` are placeholders, the app-manifest layer governance hook into `OntologyAdapter::open` is TODO'd (`permissions()` is advisory), and `AdapterError::PermissionDenied` is never emitted.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Replace placeholder gates with an ADR-012-backed implementation.
- [ ] Plumb the gate through `Substrate::subscribe_adapter` and `OntologyAdapter::open`.
- [ ] Adapters emit `AdapterError::PermissionDenied` when denied.
- [ ] Integration tests cover allow/deny paths.

## Dependencies

- ADR-012 governance work (M1.6+).

## Notes

- High-impact security/governance bead.

name: ws13: integration — implement honest affordance ∩ permit intersection in compose::honest_affordances
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 21; `compose.rs:804`; cross-cutting §2).

## Problem / gap

`AffordanceDecl` passes through composer unfiltered (ADR-006 rule 2 intersection lives in `surface_host/compose.rs:804::honest_affordances`, currently identity). Real wiring lands with M2's active-radar loop.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Implement actual intersection of declared affordances and permission grants.
- [ ] Surfaces hide affordances the user/app cannot perform.
- [ ] Tests cover allow/deny intersections.

## Dependencies

- Task 20 (governance gate).
- ADR-008 goal-aggregate (M2).

## Notes

- Honesty/UX safety bead.

name: ws13: integration — drive variant_id stamping in CanonResponse from surface binding
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 22; cross-cutting §1).

## Problem / gap

`variant_id` stamping is identity-mapped; ADR-006 head-metadata plumbing exists but doesn't drive rendering.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Surface bindings can specify `variant_id`.
- [ ] `CanonResponse` carries through the binding-derived `variant_id`.
- [ ] Tests cover variant selection by binding.

## Dependencies

- Possibly task 18 (canon types extraction).

## Notes

- Aligns with session-3 §6 variant reconciliation question.

name: ws13: integration — implement per-sensor healthcheck contract emitter
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 23; `HEALTHCHECK-CONTRACT.md`).

## Problem / gap

`HEALTHCHECK-CONTRACT.md` describes a per-sensor health shape; nothing emits it yet. Cross-cuts task 6 (adapter health) and task 9 (mic node-scoped path).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Implement `clawft-substrate::healthcheck` module per the contract.
- [ ] At least the mic adapter emits per-sensor health.
- [ ] Tests cover the per-sensor health shape.

## Dependencies

- Task 6 (adapter-health topic) and task 9 (mic path migration).

## Notes

- See task 28 below — this task subsumes that one.

name: ws13: substrate-rpc — enforce per-node-prefix write gate on substrate.publish
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 24; `JOURNALED-NODE-ESP32.md`; daemon ~2210–2920).

## Problem / gap

The `substrate.publish` handler verifies signatures but the typed prefix rule (only the owning node may write `substrate/<node-id>/*`) is not yet expressed. This is a security boundary for the ESP32 path.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Publish handler enforces that the node signing the write owns the prefix.
- [ ] Mismatched node-id/prefix rejected with a typed error.
- [ ] Integration test in `substrate_rpc.rs` covers allow/deny.

## Dependencies

- Task 9 (mic node-scoped path adoption).

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-weave/src/daemon.rs`.

name: ws13: substrate-rpc — add streaming log endpoint so kernel adapter drops poll fallback
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 25; `kernel.rs:319–321,412,437`).

## Problem / gap

The log poller is a periodic poll fallback because the daemon RPC has no streaming log endpoint (despite the topic declaring `RefreshHint::EventDriven` — declared intent ≠ runtime today). `diff_tail` Finding-1 fix is option-2 (capped-tail + capped-returns); option-1 (monotonic `seq: u64` per entry) is deferred pending the RPC change.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Daemon exposes a streaming log RPC.
- [ ] Kernel adapter drops the periodic poll fallback.
- [ ] Optional: introduce monotonic `seq: u64` per entry (option-1 fix).
- [ ] Tests cover the streaming path.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-substrate/src/kernel.rs`, `crates/clawft-weave/src/daemon.rs`.

name: ws13: substrate-rpc — test substrate.notify consumer wakeup semantics in integration suite
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 26).

## Problem / gap

The integration suite does not yet exercise consumer-wakeup semantics for `substrate.notify`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Add an integration test that confirms `substrate.notify` wakes a subscribed consumer.
- [ ] Test runs in `substrate_rpc.rs` alongside existing publish/read/subscribe coverage.

## Dependencies

- None.

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-weave/tests/substrate_rpc.rs`.

name: ws13: sensors — ship a Presence exemplar adapter
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 27; sensor-framework deferred items).

## Problem / gap

No `Presence` characterization adapter ships. Sensor-framework is exercised only by `Rate` (mic).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Implement a `Presence` exemplar adapter.
- [ ] Tests cover its emission shape.
- [ ] Documented in sensor-framework adoption notes.

## Dependencies

- Coordinates with task 10 (second Characterization exemplar) — this task is one valid choice for that exemplar.

## Notes

- May be merged with task 10 during scheduling.

name: ws13: sensors — implement HEALTHCHECK-CONTRACT.md as clawft-substrate::healthcheck module
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 28; `HEALTHCHECK-CONTRACT.md`).

## Problem / gap

The healthcheck contract has no in-tree representation; it lives only in planning docs.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Land a `clawft-substrate::healthcheck` module that codifies the contract.
- [ ] Adapters can produce contract-compliant health payloads.
- [ ] Tests validate the module's API.

## Dependencies

- Task 23 may subsume this — coordinate scheduling.

## Notes

- Effectively a duplicate of task 23 framing, retained because the audit listed both. Implementer may merge.

name: ws13: sensors — resolve legacy-flat-path vs node-scoped-path naming and ship migration
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 29; `ADOPTION.md`).

## Problem / gap

`ADOPTION.md` flags the legacy-flat-path vs node-scoped-path decision but no migration PR exists. Without resolution, mic adapter migration (task 9) and per-node write gate (task 24) cannot land cleanly.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Decide on the canonical path layout in `ADOPTION.md`.
- [ ] Land a migration PR that updates emitters and consumers.
- [ ] Document any compatibility shims and a removal date.

## Dependencies

- Blocks tasks 9 and 24.

## Notes

- `.planning/ontology/ADOPTION.md`.

name: ws13: weftos-admin — add wired Modal ("confirm restart") to admin surface
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 30).

## Problem / gap

The Modal container is only exercised in the demo lab. Need a real wired Modal in the admin surface (e.g., "confirm restart") to exercise the container outside the demo.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Add a Modal node to the admin surface fixture.
- [ ] Composer renders and dispatches affordances correctly.
- [ ] Admin e2e test exercises the modal path.

## Dependencies

- Task 12 (wire stub-leaf primitives — Modal is among the 13).

## Notes

- `crates/clawft-surface/fixtures/weftos-admin-desktop.toml`, admin e2e harness.

name: ws13: weftos-admin — migrate auto-install-from-fixture flow off web-time workaround
description_md:
## Source

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/13-app-substrate-surface.md` (task 31).

## Problem / gap

The auto-install-on-boot bundled-fixture flow relies on a `web-time` workaround instead of a real install pipeline.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Real install pipeline replaces the bundled-fixture auto-install workaround.
- [ ] Boot still ensures the Apps tab has WeftOS Admin content.
- [ ] Tests cover the new install flow.

## Dependencies

- A real install pipeline must exist.

## Notes

- Tracks the M1.5.1α follow-on item.
